game_name: Battletoads & Double Dragon: The Ultimate Team

design_philosophy.core_loop: Select from 5 playable characters (3 Battletoads or 2 Lee brothers), progress through 7 levels defeating waves of enemies using character-specific signature attacks, with level variety including side-scrolling combat, rope swinging, spaceship navigation, and vehicle sections
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (3 continues only, no password system) combined with collision-based difficulty where single-hit mistakes cascade into level resets in cooperative play
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a superhero team of martial arts warriors (humanoid toads + kung fu brothers) infiltrating an alien warship to stop an evil overlord's galactic domination plot
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Prioritizes Battletoads' engine and design philosophy over Double Dragon's style despite crossover branding; enforces shared consequences in co-op (both players restart level if one loses all lives); offers character choice but not difficulty scaling; commitment to pre-determined pattern execution over adaptive AI

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with character death on enemy collision; if one player in cooperative mode loses all lives, both players must restart the level
difficulty.continues_system: 3 continues total for entire game with no password/save system; highly restrictive continue economy forces efficient progression
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing only; no mid-level saves; must complete each of 7 levels without continuing to progress permanently
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal difficulty adjustment; players can select from 5 characters with different move sets (toads emphasize grapple/smash attacks vs. Lee brothers emphasize spin/knee techniques) but no explicit difficulty modes documented

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level progression from spaceship exterior (level 1: Colossus tail) → interior corridors (level 2) → ship base (level 3) → external spacecraft combat (level 4: transitions to shooter genre) → missile hull infiltration (level 5) → dual boss gauntlet (levels 6-7: Shadow Boss then Dark Queen)
replayability_hooks: 5 character roster with distinct move sets encourages replays with different characters
Two cooperative modes: '2 Players A' (players can damage each other) vs. '2 Players B' (cooperative only)
Character matchup variation (example: Pimple vs. Roper boss fight differs by character selection)
High difficulty and 3-continue limit incentivizes mastery runs
Genre shift in level 4 (beat-em-up to shooter) provides mechanical novelty

world_building_techniques: Opening exposition: Dark Queen flees after previous Battletoads defeat, returns allied with Double Dragon's Shadow Warriors
Environmental progression: Player travels through alien spaceship Colossus from exterior to interior to destruction
Crossover justification: Narrative framing of why Battletoads recruit Lee brothers ('decide to even the odds')
Visual level design: Stage sequence mirrors plot escalation (infiltration → combat → escape → final bosses)
Character presence: 5 playable protagonists bring their respective franchises' identities into shared conflict

boss_design.philosophy: Signature villain encounters tied to narrative climax; final two stages devoted entirely to boss battles (Shadow Boss in stage 6, Dark Queen in stage 7); bosses serve as both mechanical obstacles and story-driven confrontations against crossover antagonists
notable_bosses: Shadow Boss (stage 6 boss) - Survived ship destruction, requires assault on escape missile hull
Dark Queen (stage 7 final boss) - Primary antagonist returning with new galactic scheme; concludes both Battletoads and Double Dragon narrative threads
Roper/Willy (stage-specific variant) - Double Dragon boss character recontextualized; example: Pimple fights Roper in SNES version, suggesting character-matchup variations

faction_indicators: Battletoads faction: Zitz, Pimple, Rash (humanoid amphibian warriors with signature 'Smash Hit' attacks)
Double Dragon faction: Billy and Jimmy Lee (young martial arts expert brothers with Dragon Force spin kick techniques)
Antagonist faction: Dark Queen (evil overlord from Battletoads lore) + Shadow Warriors (Double Dragon series villains)
Authority: Earth's military (neutralized at game start, establishing stakes)
danger_signals: Giant spaceship Colossus emerging from Moon signals existential threat
Dark Queen's return after prior defeat indicates escalating villainy
Fusion of two villain factions (Dark Queen + Shadow Warriors) represents unprecedented collaborative threat
Mission structure: infiltration of alien vessel rather than defense of territory indicates asymmetric warfare scenario
environmental_storytelling: Level 1: Colossus exterior (spaceship tail) - establishes alien invasion aesthetic
Level 2: Interior corridors - reveals ship's scale and complexity
Level 3: Ship base - approaches power source/weak point
Level 4: Exterior combat via spacecraft - expands beyond single environment, shows galactic scope
Level 5: Missile hull assault - visual transition showing escape vessel and escalating danger
Levels 6-7: Boss arena isolation - narrative focus tightens to final confrontations
